raw_consumer_statement:
My credit card with CITI was used exclusively for XXXX purchases beginning XXXX XXXX XXXX  totaling {$6800.00} of fraud as shown per my transactions. I do not have an XXXX account associated or linked to any XXXX account with my credit card. I decided to do a budget mid XXXX and noticed I've been paying thousands of extra each month and I realized there's fraud being committed. I disputed these charges at once as I did not authorize nor purchase any of these items. 

They issued me a new card and gave me conditional credit. A few weeks later they are saying I'm responsible for the majority of these unauthorized purchases. They have reversed the conditional credit for 99 % of the purchases and now expect me to pay it back. You can clearly see that my card was hacked per the documents they've released. The items purchased are all from XXXX and going to different people in the USA. Citi has provided the addresses and the individuals who the packages are getting delivered too. 
The first transaction occurred on XX/XX/year> for {$9.00} with the last transaction was XX/XX/XXXX for {$33.00}. 
A possible solution they provide is for you to send a email disputing with a claim number for each individual dispute with the form they provide as well as evidence that it's not you who used the card. It puts me at a roadblock because in the instructions they also say I can't send screenshots, photos, or zip files of how i will dispute because their email can't handle that. So how do I get my evidence over? I have called over 4 times to reach the fraud department about this issue and each time I get bounced around to different individuals who don't have a consistent answer to my problem.
